description: Thamara Damayanthi Wickramasinghe Petitioner Vs. Chairman of the Debt Conciliation Board and Others Thamara Damayanthi Wickramasinghe Petitioner Vs. Chairman of the Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Thamara Damayanthi Wickramasinghe Petitioner Vs. Chairman of the Debt Conciliation Board and Others Thamara Damayanthi Wickramasinghe Petitioner Vs. Chairman of the Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner challenges the decisions taken by the Debt Conciliation Board at three stages; namely, at the stage of accepting the application by a debtor after 21 years of the impugned deed of transfer, the stage of settling the disputing or issuance of the certificate in terms of Section 29(4), and the decision taken to refuse to revisit the decision already made in terms of Section 51(4) of the Ordinance. Held – the Petitioner is entitled to relief as sought since the Board failed to follow the rules of natural justice, consider the attended circumstances, the passage of time from the date of the transfer till the date of the application, and the refusal to revisit their own decision based on irrelevant material.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
